description: reconcileFluxAndHelm honors ALGA_APPLIANCE_KUBECONFIG / in-cluster config instead of the hardcoded /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml
An app-channel update completes through the flux/helm reconcile from inside the control-plane pod (no '/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml' error)
Sidebar 'Setup' entry renamed to 'Manage' and changed from <a href="/setup/"> to an in-SPA section switch (no /setup navigation)
Manage section renders with sub-tabs: Updates, Control-plane, License, Settings
Sub-tab switching within Manage is client-side state (no page reload)
Manage area and all its endpoints are gated behind the existing AuthGate / host-service session
First-run wizard (/setup, mode=setup) is unaffected; Manage appears only post-install (mode=status) and the dead-link behavior is gone
GET /api/manage/status returns current app version + installed channel
GET /api/manage/status returns running control-plane digest, resolved channel controlPlane digest, and an upgradeAvailable flag
GET /api/manage/status returns current edition + license expiry
GET /api/manage/status returns current app URL + DNS settings
GET /api/manage/status requires auth
Manage client fetches /api/manage/status and renders per-tab state with loading and error handling
Updates tab shows the current app version and installed channel
Update button triggers POST /api/updates within the installed channel (no channel selector)
Updates tab polls install-state and reflects running -> complete/blocked
Updates tab shows 'up to date' vs 'update available' (best-effort version compare)
Update blocked/failure surfaces the reason clearly
host-agent adds POST /v1/control-plane/upgrade

host-agent upgrade route runs the bootstrap control-plane apply (resolve channel controlPlane digest -> ctr pull -> kubectl apply -k overlay), reusing bootstrap-control-plane.sh / resolve-control-plane-image.mjs, and returns without blocking on the pod Recreate
host-service POST /api/control-plane/upgrade forwards to the host-agent over the socket and returns 202
POST /api/control-plane/upgrade requires auth
Control-plane tab shows running digest vs resolved channel digest (upgrade available?)
Control-plane tab shows 'up to date' and disables/no-ops the upgrade button when current (idempotent)
After triggering an upgrade, the UI shows 'upgrading, reconnecting...' and polls health/status until the new pod answers
UI confirms the new control-plane digest after reconnect
Upgrade failure surfaces the recovery hint (alga-control-plane-reapply); no auto-rollback in v1
POST /api/license/apply validates the airgap license JWS format (reuse server.mjs check)
POST /api/license/apply updates the appliance-license-seed secret (LICENSE_TOKEN + EDITION_CHOICE)
POST /api/license/apply rollout-restarts the app to pick up the new license
POST /api/license/apply requires auth
Invalid license JWS is rejected with a clear error and no secret mutation
License tab shows current edition + license expiry (decoded from seed/JWS)
POST /api/settings/app-url rewrites appUrl/host/domainSuffix in the alga-core values configmap
POST /api/settings/app-url persists release-selection.json runtime.appHostname (+ DNS) to /var/lib
POST /api/settings/app-url reconciles the alga-core HelmRelease so NEXTAUTH_URL re-renders
POST /api/settings/app-url requires auth
app-url input is validated/normalized (reuse appUrlFromInput/hostFromAppUrl; full http://host:port supported)
Settings tab shows the current app URL + DNS and supports system vs custom DNS resolvers (mirrors setup DNS handling)
Each mutating action (update, upgrade, license apply, settings apply) shows a confirmation step plus in-progress and result/failure states
